*�����������������!debugControl

Author: Ivan DelSol

This component provides an interface for formatting the TouchDesigner "debug" function. It can be used for experimenting with styles and can set the startup mode of "debug" by leaving it in your project with Apply Settings On Start parameter set to True.

*������������������|def onValueChange(par, prev):
	ext.DocsHelper.onParValueChange(par, prev)

def onPulse(par):

*������������������l"""
Generic docs help button and pulse par system
"""

DocsHelper = op.TDResources.mod.DocsHelper.DocsHelper

2
*����������������� 8"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner components with python. An
extension is accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from any operator
within the extended component. If the extension is promoted via its
Promote Extension parameter, all its attributes with capitalized names
can be accessed externally, e.g. op('yourComp').PromotedFunction().

Help: search "Extensions" in wiki
"""

from TDStoreTools import StorageManager
TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions
menuAttrs = ['printStyle']
toggleAttrs = ['showDAT', 'showFunction', 'showLineNo', 'suppress']
strAttrs = ['formatOverride', 'functionOverride', 'timeStamp']

# local testing
# tdu.debug = mod('../debug')
# debug = tdu.debug.debug

class DebugControlExt:
	"""
	DebugControlExt description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		if not ownerComp.par.Applysettingsonstart.eval():
			try:
				self.Refresh()
			except:
				pass

	def Refresh(self):
		for attr in toggleAttrs + menuAttrs:
			setattr(self.ownerComp.par, attr.capitalize(), 
											getattr(tdu.debug.style, attr))
		self.ownerComp.par.Formatoverride = tdu.debug.style.format

	def Test(self):
		val = self.ownerComp.evalExpression(self.ownerComp.par.Testdebug.eval())
		debug(val)

	def onParValueChange(self, par, prev):
		if par.name in \
				[a.capitalize() for a in menuAttrs + toggleAttrs + strAttrs]:
			self.Apply(par)

	def Apply(self, par=None):
		argDict = {}
		functionOverride = self.ownerComp.evalExpression(
									self.ownerComp.par.Functionoverride.eval())
		argDict['functionOverride'] = functionOverride
		standardAttrs = menuAttrs + toggleAttrs + strAttrs
		standardAttrs.remove('functionOverride')
		for attr in standardAttrs:
			if par is None or par.name == attr.capitalize():				
				argDict[attr] = \
						getattr(self.ownerComp.par, attr.capitalize()).eval() \
											if par is None else par.eval()
		tdu.debug.setStyle(**argDict)
		self.Refresh()

	def onParPulse(self, par):
		if par.name == 'Refresh':
			self.Refresh()
		elif par.name == 'Test':
			self.Test()
